codeReview.summary: RE-RUN review (round 11). No code changes since prior review. Blocking issue PERSISTS: MemoryQuery.search() does NOT filter by source_episode_id despite the field being added to MemoryQuery. Additionally, store() does NOT persist the provenance fields (source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context) to the database.

issues.description: search() method does NOT filter by source_episode_id. The MemoryQuery struct has a source_episode_id field (traits.rs:265) but the search SQL query (lib.rs:684) never filters on this field. Only filters on: repository, is_stale, query_text, block_types, labels, language, task_type. Missing: source_episode_id filter. This contradicts 'Query memories by provenance' in expectedBehavior.

store() method does NOT persist provenance fields. The INSERT statement only includes: id, block_type, label, content, embedding, created_at, updated_at, metadata, repository, language, task_type, last_reinforcement, is_stale. Missing: source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context. These fields are read back correctly in row_to_entry() but never written during store().

summary: RE-RUN review (round 11) - status: FAIL. The blocking issue from round 10 PERSISTS: MemoryQuery.search() does NOT filter by source_episode_id. Additionally, a second blocking issue was found: store() does not persist the provenance fields. No code changes were made since prior review. The feature's core functionality is broken.
